question: What is frequency drift?

choices.A: Unintended and arbitrary offset of an oscillator from its nominal frequency
choices.B: It is caused by changes in temperature, voltage or changes in component values
choices.C: measured either in the long-term period of time
choices.D: All of the above choices

question: What is frequency tolerance?

choices.A: Initial deviation of a crystal or oscillato frequency as compared to the absolute at 25 deg. C.
choices.B: Detuning of receiver front-end caused by overload
choices.C: Ability to recover from frequency changes
choices.D: Ability to tolerate shock and vibration

question: What does the term "Frequency Stability Over Temperature" means?

choices.A: Frequency tolerance with resprect to a range of temperature measured in PPM or Parts Per Million
choices.B: The higher the PPM value the better is the stability
choices.C: This specification is intended for RF amplifiers
choices.D: It measures the gain with respect to temperature

question: What does the term "Selectivity"means?

choices.A: It pertains to the selection of components in a circuit
choices.B: It is a process where majority carriers are selected in a semiconductor material
choices.C: A term that refers to the selection of channels in a transmitter
choices.D: It is the ability of the receiver to distiguish between a signal at a desired frequency and signals at a adjacent frequencies

question: What does the term "Receiver Sensitivity" means?

choices.A: It is a measure of a receiver's ability to re-produce very weak signals
choices.B: It is a measure of a receiver's abiliy to re-produce the modulationexactly as it was transmitted
choices.C: It is the ability of a receiver to overcome strong signals
choices.D: It is the ability of a receiver to discriminate against off frequency signals

question: What safety equipment item should you always add at home built equipment that is powered from 230 volt AC lines?

choices.A: A fuse or circuit breaker in series with the equipment
choices.B: A fuse or circuit breaker is parallel with the equipment
choices.C: Install Zener diodes across AC inputs
choices.D: House the equipment in a plastic or other non-conductive enclosure

question: What is the most important safety precaution to take when putting up an antenna tower?

choices.A: Install steps on your tower for safe climbing
choices.B: Insulate the base of the tower to avoid lightning strikes
choices.C: Ground the base of the tower to avoid lightning strikes
choices.D: Look for and stay clear of any overhead electrical wires

question: To avoid eccessively high human exposure to RF fields, how should amateur antennas generally be mounted?

choices.A: With a high current point near ground
choices.B: As far away from accessible areas as possible
choices.C: On a non-metallic mast
choices.D: With the elements in a horizontal position

question: What are the frequency limits of the 2-meter band in ITU Region 3?

choices.A: Be sure no one can activate the transmitter
choices.B: Disconnect the antenna-positioning mechanism
choices.C: Point the antenna away from the sun so it doesn't concentrate solar energy on you
choices.D: Be sure you and the antenna structure are properly grounded

question: If you are the net conrol station of a daily HF net, what should you do if the frequency on which you normally meet is in use just before the net begins?

choices.A: Reduce your output power and start the net as usual
choices.B: Increase your power output so that the net participants will be able to hear you over the existing activity
choices.C: Conduct the net on a clear frequency 3 to 5 kHz away from the regular net frequency
choices.D: Cancel the net for the day

question: What is the purpose of the VOX sensitivity control?

choices.A: To set the timing of transmitting activation
choices.B: To set the audio frequency range at which the transmitter activates
choices.C: To set the audio level at which the transmitter activates
choices.D: None of these choices is correct

question: What is the best instrument to use to check the signal quality of a CW or single-sideband phone transmitter?

choices.A: A monitoring oscilloscope
choices.B: A field-strength meter
choices.C: A sidetone monitor
choices.D: A signal tracer and an audio amplifier

question: Which of the following statements about station grounding is true?

choices.A: The chassis of each piece of station equipment should be tied together with high-impedance conductors
choices.B: If the chassis of all station equiment is connected with a good conductor, there is no need to tie them to an earth ground
choices.C: RF hot spots can occur in a station located above the ground floor if the equipment is grounded by a long ground wire
choices.D: A ground loop is an effective way to ground station equipment

question: If a single-sideband phone transmitter is 100% modulated, what will a speech processor do to the transmitter's power?

choices.A: It will increase the output PEP
choices.B: It will add nothing to the output PEP
choices.C: It will decrease the peak output power
choices.D: It will decrease the average power output

question: Why is it the best not to draw the DC power for a 100-Watt HF transcieverfrom an automobile's cigarette lighter socket?

choices.A: the is not wired with an RF-shielded power cable
choices.B: The socket's wiring may not not be adequate for the current being drawn by the tranciever
choices.C: The DC polarity of the socket reversed from the polarity of the modern HF transciever
choices.D: The power from the socket is never adequately filtered for HF transciever operation

question: Which of the following is an advantage of a switched-mode power supply as compared to a linear power supply?

choices.A: Higfher output voltages are possible with the switched-mode supply
choices.B: Fewer cercuit components are required for the switch-mode power supply
choices.C: The relative high frequency power oscillator allows the use of small, light. Weight and low-cost transformer in the switch-modesupply
choices.D: All of the above

question: What would be the SWR if you feed a vertical antenna that has 25-ohm feed point impedance with 50-ohm coaxial cable?

choices.D: You cannot determine SWR from impedance values
